{"query":"Shohei Ohtani 2026 ESPY Awards specific categories won list","model":"mako","response_time_ms":838,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"Other multiple winners were A'ja Wilson of the Las Vegas Aces, who won best athlete, women's sports and best WNBA player; Shohei Ohtani of the Los Angeles Dodgers, who won best single-game performance and best MLB player; and Myles Garrett, who was traded from the Cleveland Browns to the Los Angeles Rams on June 1.2 days ago"},"results":[{"url":"https://www.billboard.com/music/awards/new-york-knicks-sweep-2026-espys-full-winners-list-1236295681/#:~:text=Other%20multiple%20winners%20were%20A,Angeles%20Rams%20on%20June%201.","title":"New York Knicks Sweep 2026 ESPYS: Full Winners List","content":"One month after The New York Knicks won the 2026 NBA Finals, ending a 53-year championship drought for the franchise, the team and its star players took a victory lap by sweeping the 2026 ESPY Awards.
The Knicks won the ESPY for best team; Jalen Brunson won best athlete, men’s sports; best championship performance; and best NBA player; and teammate OG Anunoby’s tip-in in the NBA finals won for best play.
Here’s the complete list of 2026 ESPYS nominations, with winners marked.
Tyce Armstrong, Baylor Baseball — hit three grand slams in a single game, tying a record set 50 years ago"},{"url":"https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/2026/07/15/2026-espys-live-updates-winners-highlights-marcello-hernandez/90922347007/","title":"2026 ESPYs award winners and highlights from show","content":"NEW YORK – Top athletes, musicians and Hollywood stars gathered to recognize the best from the past year in sports on Wednesday, July 15, for ESPN's 2026 ESPYs.
It marks the second time the Las Vegas Aces star has taken home the award, previously winning it in 2024.
Wilson has averaged 25.5 points, 9.8 rebounds and 2.9 assists per game across 22 contests thus far during the 2026 WNBA season.
Garrett's girlfriend, Olympic medalist Chloe Kim, presented him with the award alongside comedian Tiffany Haddish.
Anunoby's tip-in during the Knicks' Game 4 win over the Spurs helped New York complete a 29-point comeback and build a 3-1 series lead in an NBA Finals the team eventually won 4-1.
New York Knicks point guard Jalen Brunson took home the award for the best championship performance after he notched 45 points in his team's NBA title-clinching Game 5 win over the San Antonio Spurs.
Garrett generated an NFL-record 23 sacks for the Cleveland Browns last season and was traded to the Los Angeles Rams during the 2026 offseason for a package involving Jared Verse and multiple draft assets.
In addition to host Marcello Hernandez, award presenters will include athletes Simone Biles, Allyson Felix, Eileen Gu, Jayson Tatum and Lindsey Vonn."},{"url":"https://www.espn.com/espys/story/_/id/49359664/espys-2026-award-winners-jalen-brunson-aja-wilson-fernando-mendoza","title":"Full list of every ESPYS 2026 award winner - ESPN","content":"The 2026 ESPYS presented by Capital One celebrated the greatest athletes and moments in sports Wednesday.

Shohei Ohtani of the Los Angeles Dodgers has redefined what a two-way player can look like in the modern era of baseball. Ohtani is coming off a 2025 season in which he won his fourth MVP and second straight World Series championship. The 31-year-old has been a problem at the plate for pitchers in 2026, with the slugger tied for 13th in batting average (.295), tied for 17th in home runs (17), and fourth in OPS (.963). Ohtani's name was littered throughout the award categories, with the designated hitter leading all players with three nominations. Hannah Hidalgo of the Notre Dame Fighting Irish, who broke the record for steals in a game with 16, and Bam Adebayo of the Miami Heat, who scored 83 points in a game, round out the Best Single-Game Performance category nominees.
